slides.title: Global Storytelling and Creativity
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 8 English, Unit 8: Global Storytelling and Creativity. Welcome to Unit 8 of your Year 8 English course. This unit is called Global Storytelling and Creativity, and it is one of the most exciting units you will explore this year. Throughout this unit, you will discover how stories shape the world around us, how creative people use language to inspire and persuade, and how you can develop your own storytelling skills. You will read a powerful original story called The Edge of the Infinite, which follows a young Somali storyteller named Hannah as she competes in a national youth storytelling competition in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania. Her journey will show you that great stories do not simply entertain people; they change lives, preserve memories, and give voice to communities that might otherwise be forgotten. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Summarise the central ideas and important details in Global Storytelling and Creativity texts
Support inferences and comparisons with relevant text evidence
Use Grade 8 vocabulary accurately in discussion and writing
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to summarise the central ideas and important details in global storytelling and creativity texts. you will also support inferences and comparisons with relevant text evidence. you will also use grade 8 vocabulary accurately in discussion and writing. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: crusade, clarify, assail, infinite, motivate, stamina. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: Global Storytelling and Creativity: source text 1
Global Storytelling and Creativity: source text 2
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Global Storytelling and Creativity: source text 1 and Global Storytelling and Creativity: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: How to Form It
bullets: The structure is: Subject + has/have + been + verb-ing Use have been with I, you, we, they: I have been studying.
slides.narration: The first language focus is How to Form It. The structure is: Subject + has/have + been + verb-ing Use have been with I, you, we, they: I have been studying. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Story Retelling. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
